assignments.value:
=

High Potential Property Opportunity

Address: {{ $json.address.street }}, {{ $json.address.city }}, {{ $json.address.state }} {{ $json.address.zip }}

Property Details:

  • Market Value: ${{ $json.marketValue }}
  • Equity: {{ $json.equityPercentage }}%
  • Owner Status: {{ $json.ownerStatus }}
  • Square Feet: {{ $json.squareFeet }}
  • Bedrooms: {{ $json.bedrooms }}
  • Bathrooms: {{ $json.bathrooms }}
  • Year Built: {{ $json.yearBuilt }}
  • Last Sale Date: {{ $json.lastSaleDate }}
  • Last Sale Price: ${{ $json.lastSalePrice }}

Owner Information:

  • Owner Name: {{ $json.owner.name }}
  • Mailing Address: {{ $json.owner.mailingAddress }}
  • Phone Numbers: {{ $json.owner.phoneNumbers ? $json.owner.phoneNumbers.join(', ') : 'N/A' }}
  • Email: {{ $json.owner.email || 'N/A' }}

This property appears to be a high-potential opportunity based on:

  • High equity percentage
  • Absentee owner

nodes.name: Sticky Note Main Flow

parameters.content:
## Main Workflow Flow
This part of the workflow handles the regular scanning and processing of property data. It runs on a schedule to detect new properties or changes to existing ones, then passes the filtered results along for detailed analysis.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note Filter & Analyze

parameters.content:
## Property Filtering & Analysis
Here we filter the properties based on criteria for high-potential leads (high equity %, absentee owners, etc.) and fetch detailed information about each property to prepare comprehensive reports for the sales team.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note Notifications

parameters.content:
## Notifications
This section delivers the property leads to the sales team through multiple channels:

1. Email alerts with detailed property and owner information
2. Slack notifications for quick updates

Both include Google Maps links to quickly view the property location.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note Instructions

parameters.content:
## Setup Instructions

1. **API Keys & Credentials**:
   - Add your BatchData API Key to the BatchData API Configuration node
   - Set up SMTP credentials for email delivery
   - Configure Slack API credentials for team notifications

2. **Customize Search Parameters**:
   - Adjust property search criteria in the BatchData API Configuration node
   - Modify the filtering conditions in the Filter High Potential node

3. **Notification Recipients**:
   - Update email recipients in the Send Email Alert node
   - Set appropriate Slack channel in the Post to Slack node

assignments.name: slackMessage

parameters.content:
## Property Filtering & Analysis
Here we filter the properties based on criteria for high-potential leads (high equity %, absentee owners, etc.) and fetch detailed information about each property to prepare comprehensive reports for the sales team.

## Setup Instructions

1. **API Keys & Credentials**:
   - Add your BatchData API Key to the BatchData API Configuration node
   - Set up SMTP credentials for email delivery
   - Configure Slack API credentials for team notifications

2. **Customize Search Parameters**:
   - Adjust property search criteria in the BatchData API Configuration node
   - Modify the filtering conditions in the Filter High Potential node

3. **Notification Recipients**:
   - Update email recipients in the Send Email Alert node
   - Set appropriate Slack channel in the Post to Slack node

## Main Workflow Flow
This part of the workflow handles the regular scanning and processing of property data. It runs on a schedule to detect new properties or changes to existing ones, then passes the filtered results along for detailed analysis.

## Notifications
This section delivers the property leads to the sales team through multiple channels:

1. Email alerts with detailed property and owner information
2. Slack notifications for quick updates

Both include Google Maps links to quickly view the property location.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Schedule Market Scan".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Query BatchData Properties" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Properties" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Filter High Potential" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Property Details" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Email Alert" node (n8n-nodes-base.emailSend) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post to Slack" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Sticky Note Main Flow" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note Filter & Analyze" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note Notifications" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note Instructions" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "BatchData API Configuration".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Format Email Content".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
